vocab.meaning: phân bổ một khoản ngân sách
vocab.example: I usually allocate a modest budget for footwear each year, as I prefer quality over quantity.

vocab.meaning: mức giá đắt đỏ, cắt cổ
vocab.example: I tend to shy away from designer sneakers with an exorbitant price tag, regardless of how trendy they are.

vocab.meaning: đáng đồng tiền bát gạo, hiệu quả về chi phí
vocab.example: Investing in high-quality leather boots is more cost-effective in the long run because they last for years.

vocab.meaning: vung tiền vào việc mua sắm thứ gì đó xa xỉ
vocab.example: Every once in a while, I like to splash out on a pair of limited-edition trainers to treat myself.

vocab.meaning: trong khả năng chi trả (tài chính)
vocab.example: I always ensure that my shoe purchases are strictly within my means to avoid any financial strain.
I once paid a hefty sum for specialized running shoes, but they significantly improved my athletic performance.

vocab.meaning: người quan tâm đến thương hiệu
vocab.example: I'm not particularly brand-conscious, so I'm happy to buy affordable shoes as long as they are comfortable.
When it comes to everyday footwear, I take a more frugal approach and look for seasonal sales.
Formal shoes can be quite pricey, but they are an essential investment for professional settings.

vocab.meaning: hợp thức hóa/thấy xứng đáng với chi phí bỏ ra
vocab.example: The durability and comfort of these shoes definitely justify the expense.
